sections.label: Technique
sections.placeholder: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the prostate before and after intravenous gadolinium-based contrast. State field strength, sequences, and any diffusion/dynamic series.

sections.label: Comparison
sections.placeholder: Date and type of relevant prior imaging, if available.

sections.label: Findings
sections.placeholder:
Prostate volume (ellipsoid, mL) and PSA density (PSA/volume).
Peripheral zone (PZ): T2 signal; DWI/ADC as the dominant PZ sequence; DCE positive/negative.
Transition zone (TZ): T2 morphology as the dominant TZ sequence (encapsulated nodule vs lenticular/non-circumscribed); DWI.
Index lesion — sector on the 39-sector map (zone PZ/TZ/CZ/AFS, side, level base/mid/apex), maximum diameter (mm on ADC, or T2 for TZ), per-sequence scores, and PI-RADS v2.1 category (1-5). Note: PZ DWI-3 upgrades to 4 if DCE positive; TZ T2-3 upgrades to 4 if DWI 5; a category-4 lesion becomes 5 at >=15 mm or with extraprostatic extension.
Second/additional lesion(s) with the same descriptors.
Extraprostatic extension (capsular contact length/bulge), seminal vesicle invasion, neurovascular bundle and bladder-neck/apex/rectal involvement.
Lymph nodes (short-axis, morphology) and bones (focal marrow lesion).

sections.label: Impression
sections.placeholder: Concise prioritised bullets. Lead with the index lesion stated with its PI-RADS v2.1 category (1-5), sector/zone localisation, and maximum diameter (mm). Management implication: category 1-2 = clinically significant cancer unlikely, routine; category 3 = equivocal, consider targeted biopsy or short-interval re-imaging per local pathway; category 4-5 = targeted (+/- systematic) biopsy recommended. State extraprostatic extension or seminal vesicle invasion (staging/surgical relevance) and any suspicious nodes/bones. Report prostate volume and PSA density. Use only approved follow-up phrasing.

sections.label: Recommendations
sections.placeholder: Evidence-based follow-up or correlation, drawn from approved phrasing, only if indicated.
